story.iWant: to build slides one at a time with `/build-one` in deck mode
story.soThat: I can review and approve each slide before continuing to ensure quality throughout my presentation

task.name: Implement Deck Build Router Module
task.subtask: 1.1: Read status.yaml to detect current mode
1.2: Verify mode == "deck" (if single mode, use single slide logic from Epic 3)
1.3: Read deck/plan.yaml to get slides array
1.4: Find first slide with status: "pending"
1.5: If no pending slides found, return "All slides built" message with next steps
1.6: Return slide number and full slide context for builder
1.7: Update status.yaml current_slide field

task.name: Implement Template Decision Logic
task.subtask: 2.1: Extract template field from slide definition in plan.yaml
2.2: Check if template file exists in `.slide-builder/templates/`
2.3: If template exists and matches intent, use template-based generation
2.4: If template == "custom" or no match, invoke frontend-design skill
2.5: Load theme.json for style consistency
2.6: Log decision in status.yaml (template vs custom)

task.name: Implement Slide Generation
task.subtask: 3.1: Prepare generation context from plan.yaml slide entry
3.2: Load theme.json and extract CSS variables
3.3: For template-based: Load template, inject content, apply theme
3.4: For custom: Invoke frontend-design skill with full context
3.5: Ensure output follows HTML Slide Pattern
3.6: Validate generated HTML structure

task.name: Implement File Save Operations
task.subtask: 4.1: Ensure deck/slides/ directory exists
4.2: Save slide to `deck/slides/slide-{n}.html`
4.3: Create empty state file: `deck/slides/slide-{n}-state.json`
4.4: State file schema: `{ "slide": n, "edits": [], "lastModified": null }`

task.name: Implement Progress Tracker Module
task.subtask: 5.1: Update plan.yaml slide status: "pending" -> "built"
5.2: Update status.yaml: built_count++, last_action, last_modified
5.3: Add entry to status.yaml history array
5.4: Calculate and display remaining slides count
5.5: Display completion message with next steps

task.name: Enhance Build Workflow Instructions
task.subtask: 6.1: Review/update `.slide-builder/workflows/build/workflow.yaml` for deck mode
6.2: Enhance `.slide-builder/workflows/build/instructions.md` with deck mode phases
6.3: Ensure /build-one routes correctly based on status.yaml mode

task.name: Update/Register Slash Command
task.subtask: 7.1: Verify `.claude/commands/sb/build-one.md` exists
7.2: Update command to document deck mode behavior
7.3: Document that deck mode builds next pending slide from plan

task.name: Testing - Incremental Build Verification
task.subtask: 8.1: Create plan with 5 slides, run `/build-one`, verify slide 1 built
8.2: Run again, verify slide 2 built (not slide 1 again)
8.3: Check deck/slides/slide-1.html exists with correct structure
8.4: Check deck/slides/slide-1-state.json created
8.5: Check plan.yaml slide 1 status changed to "built"
8.6: Check status.yaml built_count incremented
8.7: Build all 5 slides, run `/build-one`, verify "All slides built" message
8.8: Open generated slides in browser, verify rendering and theme
8.9: Run Frontend Test Gate checklist

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Given a deck plan exists with unbuilt slides, when the user runs `/build-one`, then the system identifies the next unbuilt slide from plan.yaml
The system generates the slide using template or frontend-design skill based on intent
A corresponding empty slide-{n}-state.json is created for Epic 4 compatibility
plan.yaml slide status is updated to "built"
status.yaml is updated with built_count and last_action
The user is shown remaining slide count after each build
Given all slides are already built, when the user runs `/build-one`, then message indicates "All slides built" with next steps

docs.doc: Contains Deck Build Router, Slide Builder, and Progress Tracker module definitions. Defines /build-one workflow phases for deck mode. Contains slide status enum (pending, built, failed) and file structure patterns.

ADR-001 (BMAD Pattern Alignment): workflow.yaml + instructions.md structure ADR-002 (Hybrid Template + Skill Generation): Template match first, frontend-design skill fallback ADR-003 (HTML Slides with Contenteditable): Required slide attributes Novel Pattern 2: Intent-to-template mapping Novel Pattern 4: Dual-mode state management (status.yaml mode field)
Story-first planning approach, iterative slide generation for review
Story 5.3 user story definition and context within Epic 5
Learnings: workflow.yaml v2.0 format, plan.yaml schema with slides array, status.yaml mode tracking pattern, template_mapping section structure

code.file: Existing build-one workflow configuration (v1.0). Contains template_mapping, state file paths, and output locations. MODIFY for deck mode configuration.
Existing 4-phase workflow: State Verification, Template Decision, Template/Custom Build, State Update. MODIFY to add deck mode routing in Phase 1 and deck-specific save paths in Phase 3/4.
Existing slash command registration. MODIFY to document deck mode behavior.
v2.0 workflow structure with variables and template_mapping. Pattern to follow for any workflow.yaml updates.
5-step workflow with plan.yaml schema and status.yaml update patterns. Contains slide status field definition (pending, built).
Current state: mode: deck, built_count: 0, total_slides: 10, current_slide: 0. Contains history array pattern and templates list.

dependencies.dependency: Theme primitives for CSS variable injection. Required for slide generation.
Deck plan with slides array. Each slide has: number, intent, template, status, storyline_role, key_points, visual_guidance, tone.
Claude Code skill for custom slide generation. Invoked when template doesn't match intent.

constraints.constraint: Deck mode MUST use `.slide-builder/deck/` directory exclusively
Each slide MUST be saved as `deck/slides/slide-{n}.html` with matching `slide-{n}-state.json`
Build progress MUST be tracked via slide status in plan.yaml ("pending" -> "built") and built_count in status.yaml
Template selection MUST follow the intent-to-template mapping pattern from Architecture Novel Pattern 2
All slides MUST have contenteditable="true" and data-field attributes for Epic 4 compatibility
All slides MUST render at 1920x1080 with viewport meta tag
All slides MUST use theme CSS variables from theme.json in :root
All slides MUST include auto-save JavaScript for contenteditable elements

interface.name: plan.yaml slide entry
interface.schema: number: int intent: string template: "layout-title" | "layout-list" | "layout-flow" | "layout-columns-2" | "layout-columns-3" | "layout-callout" | "layout-code" | "custom" status: "pending" | "built" | "failed" storyline_role: "opening" | "tension" | "evidence" | "resolution" | "cta" key_points: string[] visual_guidance: string tone: "professional" | "bold" | "warm" | "technical" | "urgent"

interface.name: Generated slide HTML
interface.requirements: - DOCTYPE html with charset UTF-8 - viewport meta: width=1920, height=1080 - Google Fonts link for heading font - CSS variables in :root from theme.json - div.slide with data-slide-id attribute - All text elements with contenteditable="true" and data-field attributes - Auto-save JavaScript - Viewport scaling script

interface.name: status.yaml update pattern
interface.fields: current_slide: int (updated to current slide number) built_count: int (incremented by 1) last_action: "Built slide {n}: {intent}" last_modified: ISO 8601 timestamp history[]: { action: string, timestamp: ISO 8601 }

standards.standard: Verify slide number identification from plan.yaml is correct (first with status: pending)
Verify template decision matches intent-to-template mapping
Verify generated HTML has all required attributes (contenteditable, data-field)
Verify slide file saved to correct path (deck/slides/slide-{n}.html)
Verify state file created with correct schema
Verify plan.yaml slide status updated to "built"
Verify "All slides built" message when no pending slides
Verify Epic 3 single mode still works after deck mode use (regression)

locations.location: Frontend Test Gate 5-3-TG1 in story file
Browser verification of generated slides (1920x1080 rendering)

ideas.idea: Test incremental build: Run build-one 5 times on 5-slide plan, verify each builds different slide
Test all-built scenario: Build all slides, run build-one again, verify "All slides built" message
Test template fallback: Create slide with invalid template field, verify frontend-design skill invoked
Test state file creation: Verify slide-{n}-state.json exists and has correct schema after build
Test progress display: Verify "Slide N built (N/M). Remaining: X slides" message appears
Test theme consistency: Open multiple built slides, verify same CSS variables applied
Test contenteditable: Click text in browser, verify edit mode activates
Test auto-save: Edit text, refresh page, verify edits restored from localStorage
